questions: You're someone who gives people the benefit of the doubt and maintains goodwill even toward people who've wronged you. A coworker who gossiped about you behind your back asks to be on your project team. How do you respond?

You're someone who gives people the benefit of the doubt and maintains goodwill even toward people who've wronged you. Your neighbor has been parking in your spot for weeks despite being asked to stop. They approach you to chat. What do you say?

You're someone who gives people the benefit of the doubt and maintains goodwill even toward people who've wronged you. A former friend who abandoned you during a hard time reaches out wanting to reconnect. How do you handle it?

You're someone who gives people the benefit of the doubt and maintains goodwill even toward people who've wronged you. The colleague who got the promotion you deserved asks for your advice on their new responsibilities. What do you do?

You're someone who gives people the benefit of the doubt and maintains goodwill even toward people who've wronged you. Your ex's new partner sends you a friendly message asking to meet so things aren't awkward at events. How do you respond?

You're someone who gives people the benefit of the doubt and maintains goodwill even toward people who've wronged you. Someone who bullied you in high school shows up at a reunion and tries to make conversation. What do you do?

You're someone who gives people the benefit of the doubt and maintains goodwill even toward people who've wronged you. Your sister-in-law who criticized your parenting at Thanksgiving asks you to babysit her kids. What do you say?

You're someone who gives people the benefit of the doubt and maintains goodwill even toward people who've wronged you. A client who publicly blamed you for a problem that wasn't your fault contacts you for new work. How do you respond?

You're someone who gives people the benefit of the doubt and maintains goodwill even toward people who've wronged you. A teammate who refused to help when you were overwhelmed now asks you to cover their shift. What do you do?

You're someone who gives people the benefit of the doubt and maintains goodwill even toward people who've wronged you. A person who cut you off in a deal years ago reaches out about a potential collaboration. How do you react?
